8

COU!'.tTY COUNSEL 
JOHN F. CHRISTENSEN 
SENIOR DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
HARRY P. DRABKIN 
DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL 
CARL O. WAGGONER 
EPL!TY COUN TV COUNSEL 
The North San Joaquin Valley Health Systems Agency was recently 
established under Public Law 93-641 by means of a joint exercise 
of powers agreement adopted by seven counties. 
Our question 
is whether such agency is a local government agency within the 
meaning of Government Code Section 82041 and thus whether the 
agency must adopt a conflict of interest code as required by 
Government Code Section 87300. 
We have a copy of your letter dated April 1, 1977 to the County 
Counsel of San Diego County and assume that your answer will be 
the same. 
However, the governing board of the Health Systems 
Agency has asked that we ascertain that your advice will not be 
different for our agency. 
We also expect that the Agency will be designated as an area 
health planning agency pursuant to Health and Safety Code 
Section 437.7. 
You will note that health~stems agencies are 
specifically named as meeting the requirements for designation. 
Accordingly, we would like to know if your opinion will be 
different in the event of such a designation. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
Very truly yours, 
(,ilLBERT W. BOYNE

sections.question: Whether the North San Joaquin Valley Health Systems Agency, established under Public Law 93-641 by means of a joint exercise of powers agreement adopted by seven counties, is a local government agency within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041 and thus whether the agency must adopt a conflict of interest code as required by Government Code Section 87300. Additionally, whether the FPPC's opinion would be different in the event the Agency is designated as an area health planning agency pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section 437.7.
sections.question_synthetic: Is the North San Joaquin Valley Health Systems Agency, a multi-county joint powers agency established under Public Law 93-641, considered a local government agency requiring adoption of a conflict of interest code under Government Code Section 87300, and would this conclusion change if the agency is designated as an area health planning agency?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from County Counsel, not an FPPC response. Document is dated May 19, 1977 and references an April 1, 1977 FPPC letter to San Diego County Counsel regarding the same issue. No CONCLUSION or FPPC response sections present in this document. Some text is partially obscured or illegible in the header portions.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether the North San Joaquin Valley Health Systems Agency, established under Public Law 93-641 by means of a joint exercise of powers agreement adopted by seven counties, is a local government agency within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041 and thus whether the agency must adopt a conflict of interest code as required by Government Code Section 87300. Additionally, whether the FPPC's opinion would be different in the event the Agency is designated as an area health planning agency pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section 437.7.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the San Joaquin County Counsel to the FPPC asking whether a multi-county Health Systems Agency created through a joint powers agreement is a local government agency subject to conflict of interest code requirements, with a follow-up question regarding whether designation as an area health planning agency would change that determination.
